file.content:
# Review: deepmind/947.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 947`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
- [ ] **FAIL:** Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
  - The module docstring only contains the title and reference link. It should include a brief description of the problem (e.g., "There is no exact covering system with distinct moduli"). The theorem docstring has a description, but the *module* docstring does not.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos947 ... end Erdos947`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos947`).
- [x] No `open` statements needed; none present. N/A.

## 5. Custom Definitions

N/A — no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_947` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing the theorem.
- [x] Docstring references the source proof ("proved independently by Mirsky-Newman and Davenport-Rado").

### `answer()` usage

The original problem on erdosproblems.com is phrased as a direct assertion: "There is no exact covering system..." This is **Case 3** (direct assertion).

- [x] The theorem type is `¬ ∃ ...` directly — no `answer()` wrapper needed. Correct.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Value is `research solved` — valid category.
- [x] Not `research open`, so the `sorry` in the proof body is expected (solved but not formally verified).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute on the same line as `category`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number — trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` (Number Theory) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code. Appropriate for a covering systems problem.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` with `sorry` on the next line (multi-line form, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches included for this solved problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

N/A — no variants declared. The file contains only the single main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `¬`, `∃`, `ℕ`, `ℤ`, `∀`, `∣`, `↑`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (well under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified (requires `lake build`).

---

## Mathematical Review

The theorem formalizes:

> ¬ ∃ (k : ℕ) (a : Fin k → ℤ) (n : Fin k → ℕ),
>   (∀ i, 2 ≤ n i) ∧ (Function.Injective n) ∧
>   (∀ x : ℤ, ∃! i, (↑(n i) : ℤ) ∣ (x - a i))

This states: there is no finite collection of congruence classes with distinct moduli ≥ 2 such that every integer belongs to exactly one class. This matches the classical Mirsky-Newman / Davenport-Rado theorem and correctly captures the problem from erdosproblems.com/947.

**Edge cases are handled correctly:**
- `k = 0`: `∃!` over `Fin 0` is vacuously false, so excluded.
- `k = 1`: a single modulus ≥ 2 cannot cover every integer exactly once, so excluded.

The formalization is mathematically faithful.

---

## Summary

| Section | Status |
|---|---|
| 1. Copyright Header | PASS |
| 2. Import Statement | PASS |
| 3. Module Docstring | **FAIL** — missing plain-English description |
| 4. Namespace | PASS |
| 5. Custom Definitions | N/A |
| 6. Theorem Statements | PASS |
| 7. Category Attribute | PASS |
| 8. AMS Attribute | PASS |
| 9. Proof Bodies | PASS |
| 10. Variants | N/A |
| 11. Code Quality | PASS |
| 12. Build Verification | NOT VERIFIED |

**Verdict: ONE issue to fix** — add a brief plain-English description to the module docstring. For example:

```lean
/-!
# Erdős Problem 947

There is no exact covering system — that is, a finite collection of congruence classes
$a_i \pmod{n_i}$ with distinct moduli $n_i \geq 2$ such that every integer belongs to
exactly one of these congruence classes. This was proved independently by
Mirsky–Newman and Davenport–Rado.

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/947](https://www.erdosproblems.com/947)
-/
```

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 947

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/947](https://www.erdosproblems.com/947)
-/

namespace Erdos947

/--
There is no exact covering system with distinct moduli.
That is, there is no finite collection of congruence classes $a_i \pmod{n_i}$ with
distinct moduli $n_i \geq 2$ such that every integer belongs to exactly one of
these congruence classes.

This was proved independently by Mirsky-Newman and Davenport-Rado.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_947 :
    ¬ ∃ (k : ℕ) (a : Fin k → ℤ) (n : Fin k → ℕ),
      (∀ i, 2 ≤ n i) ∧
      (Function.Injective n) ∧
      (∀ x : ℤ, ∃! i : Fin k, (↑(n i) : ℤ) ∣ (x - a i)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos947
